jokes.text: the bake sale to raise money for the car wash has been canceled due to confusion
jokes.punchline: Circular logic of raising money for something that costs money
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons bureaucratic absurdity delivered with perfect economy

jokes.text: It's hard enough to keep the kids awake without you, Seymour
jokes.punchline: Teacher pointing out how boring he is
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Edna's directness and their dynamic

jokes.text: Mrs. Krabappel, I also have an announcement to make! / Can't it wait till I retire?
jokes.setup: Martin excitedly wanting to make an announcement
jokes.punchline: Krabappel's immediate deflation with retirement joke
jokes.explanation: Classic teacher burnout humor - the timing and Krabappel's consistent character voice makes this land well

jokes.text: I want one and all to join me in celebrating the anniversary of my portentous birth!
jokes.punchline: Using pretentious vocabulary for a simple concept
jokes.explanation: Perfect Martin character writing - only he would say 'portentous birth'

jokes.text: Uh, yes. Let me just say this about that
jokes.setup: Martin pretending to get a call from the president
jokes.punchline: Using Nixon's famous phrase
jokes.explanation: Good cultural reference that fits Martin's character

jokes.text: We're what now? Three? Three-and-a-half. We get beat up, but we get an explanation
jokes.punchline: Very specific and pathetic ranking system
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary on school social hierarchies with perfect specificity

jokes.text: Hey, it's Saturday! Your powers are useless against me
jokes.setup: Bart sees his teacher at a party
jokes.punchline: Treating teacher authority like supernatural powers
jokes.explanation: Perfect encapsulation of the student-teacher dynamic with superhero language

jokes.text: Hey, Houdini! Why don't you saw Martin in half? Oh, I'm not the kind of magician who does tricks. I'm a mathemagician!
jokes.punchline: Martin's proud portmanteau word
jokes.explanation: Classic Martin nerdiness with a decent pun that fits his character

jokes.text: Now, prepare to marvel at the mysteries of the universe as I make this remainder disappear. But seven goes into 28 four times. Uh- This is a magic seven!
jokes.setup: Martin doing a math magic trick
jokes.punchline: Getting called out and desperately improvising
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from confident to desperate, very Martin-esque

jokes.text: It might be nice to talk to a grown-up for a change
jokes.setup: Two adults at a children's party
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging their sad situation
jokes.explanation: Realistic teacher sentiment that sets up their bonding

jokes.text: You know, normally I spend my Saturdays carefully laying out my clothes for the following week. Then I stroll down to the car wash to see if Gus is there
jokes.punchline: Incredibly pathetic Saturday activities
jokes.explanation: Perfect Skinner characterization - methodical and lonely

jokes.text: I collect matchbooks from glamorous nightclubs. It's amazing. If you just write to them and ask them nicely-
jokes.punchline: Living vicariously through matchbook collecting
jokes.explanation: Sad but believable hobby that shows Edna's unfulfilled desires

jokes.text: Is this how you imagined your life, Edna? Well, yes. But then I was a very depressed child
jokes.punchline: Edna's matter-of-fact dark response
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor with perfect setup/punchline structure and character truth

jokes.text: Seymour! I told you not to play in the neighbor's yard! It's a party, Mother. I was invited. Well, then I must have been invited too
jokes.punchline: Her logic for crashing the party
jokes.explanation: Great mother-son dynamic showing his infantilization

jokes.text: I told you we should've served cake instead of oysters!
jokes.setup: Kids getting sick at birthday party
jokes.punchline: Revealing the bizarre menu choice
jokes.explanation: Absurd menu choice that explains the chaos and fits Martin's pretensions

jokes.text: Eh, I gave my oysters to the cat. [Cat retching sounds]
jokes.punchline: Cat is now sick instead
jokes.explanation: Smart move by Bart with visual payoff

jokes.text: Hey, why'd you eat 'em? I thought you were a vegetarian. I didn't. I just wanted to leave
jokes.setup: Lisa appearing to break her vegetarianism
jokes.punchline: She faked being sick to escape
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Lisa's intelligence and social awareness

jokes.text: More tea, Edna? What kind of little boy has a tea set? I think we both know the answer to that. A lucky boy!
jokes.setup: Question about Martin having a tea set
jokes.punchline: Unexpected positive spin on obvious answer
jokes.explanation: Clever misdirection that subverts expectations about Martin

jokes.text: You know, Seymour, there's an innocence about you that can be quite charming
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment about his naivety
jokes.explanation: Good character development showing Edna's attraction to his innocence

jokes.text: I've always admired your tart honesty and ability to be personally offended by broad social trends
jokes.punchline: Very specific and accurate character observation
jokes.explanation: Perfect character writing that captures Edna's personality precisely

jokes.text: And your laugh. You mean, 'Ha'? Mm-hmm. That's the one that makes me feel-
jokes.punchline: Her deadpan, unenthusiastic demonstration
jokes.explanation: Perfect Edna moment - her cynical 'Ha' contrasts beautifully with his romance

jokes.text: You have a breath mint I could borrow? I've got some menthol cigarettes. Oh, the heck with it!
jokes.setup: Skinner asking for breath freshener before kissing
jokes.punchline: Edna's solution and then abandoning all pretense
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing both characters' practical and romantic sides

jokes.text: Good gravy! Oh, thank you. It's just brown and water
jokes.punchline: Marge's literal interpretation and sad description
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer/Marge dynamic with great misdirection and economy

jokes.text: You could've at least said you were giving her C.P.R., or rehearsing a play. Is it too late to say that? Mm-hmm
jokes.setup: Bart catching them kissing and their weak excuse
jokes.punchline: Bart offering better lies and their hopeful response
jokes.explanation: Great reversal where the kid is more clever than the adults

jokes.text: Bart, if life has taught me one lesson repeatedly, it's to know when I'm beaten
jokes.punchline: His resigned acceptance of defeat as life philosophy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Skinner characterization - his lifetime of defeat condensed into wisdom

jokes.text: your permanent record will one day disqualify you from all but the hottest and noisiest jobs
jokes.setup: Threatening Bart with his permanent record
jokes.punchline: Specific description of terrible jobs
jokes.explanation: Good specificity in describing blue-collar work without being mean-spirited

jokes.text: This is a great day for forbidden love
jokes.setup: Bart making a deal to stay quiet
jokes.punchline: His grandiose announcement about the situation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bart's dramatic flair

jokes.text: Mmm, this dessert is exquisite. What do you call it? Applesauce. Of course. I'm sorry, I don't get out to restaurants much
jokes.punchline: It's just applesauce and his embarrassed realization
jokes.explanation: Perfect Skinner moment showing his inexperience and tendency to overcomplicate

jokes.text: Usually, it's just soup for one, salad for one, wine for three
jokes.setup: Edna explaining she doesn't entertain much
jokes.punchline: Revealing her drinking problem with mathematical precision
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor with perfect structure - the escalating portions reveal her alcoholism

jokes.text: I always thought I'd fall for a woman just like Mother. Even though I didn't want to. And now that I haven't I've discovered what true happiness can be
jokes.punchline: Backwards logic about not following Freudian expectations
jokes.explanation: Good psychological insight wrapped in Skinner's awkward delivery

jokes.text: I'm afraid I already swallowed it while you were talking
jokes.setup: Edna asking him to swallow and kiss her
jokes.punchline: His literal-minded timing issue
jokes.explanation: Classic Skinner awkwardness ruining a romantic moment

jokes.text: Good morning, students. Way to go there, Wendell! There's Ralph. He knows the score! Jimbo, how's that hat today? Janie, Janie, Bo-phaney!
jokes.punchline: Increasingly bizarre enthusiasm including rhyming
jokes.explanation: Great escalating absurdity showing how romance has changed cynical Edna

jokes.text: Oh, heck! Life's too short for fire safety. Let's go outside and pick wildflowers
jokes.punchline: Abandoning curriculum for romantic whimsy
jokes.explanation: Perfect contrast between responsible teaching and romantic distraction

jokes.text: Superintendent Chalmers! Uh, why, we're-we're on a, uh, a-a what do you call it? Field trip! With a student! Yes! He's purchasing refreshments in the lobby!
jokes.punchline: Increasingly desperate improvised lies
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of panic and lies when caught

jokes.text: Car keys! Car keys! Car keys!
jokes.setup: Skinner needing to find Bart quickly
jokes.punchline: Desperate repetitive searching
jokes.explanation: Simple panic comedy that works for the situation

jokes.text: I collect pictures of cakes that I clip out of the magazines. It all started in 1941, when Good Housekeeping featured a photo of a lovely cake
jokes.setup: Agnes explaining her hobby to Bart
jokes.punchline: Incredibly specific and sad obsession with detailed origin story
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly pathetic character detail that explains so much about the Skinner family

jokes.text: You wouldn't happen to have any real cakes around here, would you? Oh, my, no! I don't care for cake. Too sweet
jokes.punchline: She collects cake pictures but hates actual cake
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony that reveals the absurdity of her hobby and character

jokes.text: At my age, I don't have much saliva left, so you'll have to lick my thumb before I can turn the page.
jokes.punchline: Grotesquely inappropriate request for help with basic task
jokes.explanation: Brilliant cringe comedy combining aging issues with social inappropriateness

jokes.text: At my age, I don't have much saliva left so you'll have to lick my thumb before I can turn the page
jokes.punchline: Disgusting and bizarre request
jokes.explanation: Utterly bizarre and memorable request that's both disgusting and funny

jokes.text: You can't have that one! That's a coconut cake!
jokes.setup: Bart picking a cake picture to take home
jokes.punchline: Her protective reaction to specific cake type
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of her obsession with specific preferences

jokes.text: I love you, Edna Krabappel. [Class laughs] Bart's gonna marry the teacher! Where are you registered, Simpson?
jokes.setup: Bart forced to say embarrassing message
jokes.punchline: Class taking it seriously with wedding joke
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment with realistic kid reaction

jokes.text: you must promise not to fall in love with me. [Students laugh] Now Martin's scoring off me. Oh, that is it!
jokes.setup: Martin making a joke about Bart
jokes.punchline: Bart's breaking point when Martin gets laughs
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Bart can't stand being out-joked by Martin

jokes.text: Okay, crew. Set your faces to stunned
jokes.setup: Bart leading kids to see Skinner and Krabappel
jokes.punchline: Directing them like actors in a scene
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta moment showing Bart's media awareness and manipulation skills

jokes.text: And then Bart opened the door, and Principal Skinner and Mrs. Krabappel were kissing! [Gasps] And swearing!
jokes.punchline: Adding 'swearing' to make it more scandalous
jokes.explanation: Good kid logic of what makes something extra naughty

jokes.text: Father! Uncle Apu! A teacher was in the closet with the principal. And he had as many arms as Vishnu. And they were all very busy
jokes.punchline: Hindu mythology reference making it more dramatic
jokes.explanation: Brilliant cultural specificity and escalation through mythological comparison

jokes.text: They were naked in the closet together. Wait a minute. Bart's teacher is named Krabappel? I've been calling her Crandall. Why didn't someone tell me? Oh, I've been making an idiot out of myself!
jokes.punchline: More concerned about getting teacher's name wrong
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - missing the point spectacularly with misplaced priorities

jokes.text: Mrs. Krabappel and Principal Skinner were in the closet making babies. And I saw one of the babies. And the baby looked at me
jokes.punchline: Adding impossible and creepy detail about seeing a baby
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ralph absurdity with escalating impossibility and his characteristic strange details

jokes.text: Nelson, Nelson, Nelson. How many kids have you beaten up this year? I don't know, 50? Ah, 50. Where does the time go?
jokes.punchline: Treating violence statistics nostalgically
jokes.explanation: Dark humor treating school violence with administrative nostalgia

jokes.text: Bart, this is your father! Do you know where the remote is? I looked all over the house. [Shouting] Did you check your pocket? [Turns off bullhorn] It was in my pocket
jokes.setup: Homer using bullhorn for emergency communication
jokes.punchline: Using it for mundane TV remote question and immediate resolution
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - misusing emergency equipment for trivial concerns

jokes.text: I have a bomb! [Gasps] Hey, wait a minute! Those are hot dogs! Armour Hot Dogs! What kind of man wears Armour Hot Dogs?
jokes.setup: Bart trying to get attention with fake bomb
jokes.punchline: Revealed as hot dogs with brand-specific confusion
jokes.explanation: Decent visual gag with brand name confusion

jokes.text: Sex Cauldron? I thought they closed that place down!
jokes.punchline: Homer thinking they're talking about a specific establishment
jokes.explanation: Legendary Simpsons line - perfect Homer logic with incredible name and delivery

jokes.text: Well, everyone the fact is, I haven't ever had relations. I am a virgin
jokes.setup: Skinner trying to clear his name
jokes.punchline: Admitting his virginity publicly
jokes.explanation: Major character revelation that explains so much about Skinner

jokes.text: Well, Seymour, it's clear you've been falsely accused because no one, anywhere, ever would pretend to be a 44-year-old virgin
jokes.punchline: Logic that no one would fake such an embarrassing thing
jokes.explanation: Perfect logic that's both insulting and vindicating

jokes.text: We can't continue on like this trying to build a private relationship in public- the whole town watching our every move with a fine-tooth comb
jokes.punchline: Mixed metaphor of watching with a comb
jokes.explanation: Mild malapropism that works for character but isn't exceptional

jokes.text: That's why I love elementary school, Edna. The children believe anything you tell them. [Both laughing]
jokes.setup: Skinner explaining a life lesson to Bart
jokes.punchline: Revealing they're lying to the kids and are still together
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark ending that subverts everything and shows their manipulation of the children
